title: LIP-LOSS-001 reverse NCE 0.0 MBPP64 train-set in-sample bridge evaluation
interpretation: Turning lambda_reverse_nce from 0.5 to 0.0 substantially improved in-sample reconstruction, cosine diagonal, norm ratio, and energy drift relative to LIP-EVAL-027.
In-sample retrieval_top1 remained 0.96875 and retrieval_top5 remained 1.0.
In-sample diagonal margin decreased, indicating less pair-separation pressure.
The result suggests reverse NCE was the main source of positive-target geometry degradation in earlier LIP-LOSS-001 runs.
This remains latent-space diagnostic evaluation only.
known_limitations: The evaluation uses the training bundle and is in-sample.
The evaluation is latent-space only and does not evaluate text-level semantic fidelity.
The result does not establish semantic transfer, model-to-model alignment, generalization, or production readiness.
data_policy: No generated eval outputs, checkpoints, shards, bundles, datasets, zips, caches, or run artifacts are committed.
scientific_claim_status: Latent-space diagnostic evaluation only. No semantic transfer, text-level fidelity, model-to-model alignment, generalization, or production readiness claim.
